{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Field Sales Representative",
  "description": "Numac Store is a dynamic wholesale company focused on delivering a wide range of essential products, offering customers an extensive selection of food items and beverages.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Sales Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Rumuomasi, Port Harcourt - Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Responsible for identifying potential customers, promoting products, and closing sales in market locations. Commission paid on sales.


Responsibilities


	Visit markets and surrounding areas to identify and approach potential customers
	Promote, market, and sell company products directly to customers
	Build and maintain good relationships with customers to encourage repeat sales


Requirements


	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Confidence in approaching people and selling products
	Previous sales experience is an advantage but not required.


Salary
N60,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;numacstore@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Field Sales Representative Job at Numac Store




        
    

        
        Numac Store is a dynamic wholesale company focused on delivering a wide range of essential products, offering customers an extensive selection of food items and beverages.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Sales Representative

Job Location: Rumuomasi, Port Harcourt - Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Responsible for identifying potential customers, promoting products, and closing sales in market locations. Commission paid on sales.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Visit markets and surrounding areas to identify and approach potential customers
	Promote, market, and sell company products directly to customers
	Build and maintain good relationships with customers to encourage repeat sales


Requirements


	Good communication and interpersonal skills
	Confidence in approaching people and selling products
	Previous sales experience is an advantage but not required.


Salary
N60,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Chief Operating Officer (COO)",
  "description": "ISN Medical is the leading supplier of Medical Diagnostic Products and Services in Nigeria. Our mission is to give healthcare practitioners and patients peace of mind through innovative medical diagnostic solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Operating Officer (COO)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

About the Job


	The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is responsible for driving operational excellence across ISN Medical&rsquo;s core business functions, ensuring efficient delivery of products and services while enabling sustainable growth and superior customer experience.
	The role requires a strategic leader who can balance immediate operational demands with long-term transformation, particularly within a regulated healthcare and medical equipment environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee and monitor operations across Supply Chain (Demand &amp; Supply Planning, Regulatory Management, Procurement, Warehouse Management, Logistics), Sales Operations, and Customer Experience
	Ensure seamless integration and efficiency across all operational functions to support business objectives
	Drive excellence in supply chain performance and customer experience delivery
	Translate corporate strategy into clear, actionable operational execution plans
	Balance short-term operational priorities with long-term strategic growth initiatives
	Drive business growth, market expansion, and operational scalability across diverse markets
	Ensure full adherence to ISO standards (ISO 9001, ISO 13485) and regulatory requirements
	Anticipate and respond to industry trends, regulatory changes, and technological advancements
	Drive data-driven decision-making, leveraging dashboards and operational analytics
	Optimize inventory management, demand planning, procurement, and distribution efficiency
	Ensure operational resilience in delivering medical products and services in regulated environments
	Partner with Sales leadership to drive revenue growth, pricing optimization, and margin improvement
	Establish and monitor performance frameworks (KPIs, OKRs, SLAs) across all operational units
	Build and lead high-performing, cross-functional teams across operations
	Drive leadership development, capability building, and succession planning
	Ensure alignment between operational execution and commercial objectives


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, Engineering, Finance, or a related discipline. Minimum of Second Class Upper (2:1) for Bachelor&rsquo;s degree holders or Upper Credit for HND holders is required.
	14 &ndash;20+ years of progressive experience in operations leadership, with at least 8 years at senior or executive management level (Director/GM/VP level).
	A Master&rsquo;s degree (MBA or equivalent) is strongly preferred.
	Professional certifications in Operations, Supply Chain, Quality Management (ISO 13485), or Project Management are an advantage.
	Demonstrated experience managing multi-functional operations and P&amp;L. Experience working in Healthcare, medical diagnostics, medical equipment, pharma or regulated environments and dealing with OEM partners
	Proven track record in driving revenue growth, margin improvement, operational scale, and customer experience excellence.


Must have skills:


	Strategic Leadership &amp; Vision
	Operational Excellence &amp; Process Management
	People &amp; Leadership Development
	Financial Acumen &amp; Resource Management
	Regulatory Compliance &amp; Quality Assurance
	Results Orientation
	Technology &amp; Digital Transformation
	Stakeholder Management &amp; Communication
	Integrity and Ethics.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-10",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-11",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "ISN Medical Incorporated"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

}



Chief Operating Officer (COO) Job at ISN Medical




        
    

        
        ISN Medical is the leading supplier of Medical Diagnostic Products and Services in Nigeria. Our mission is to give healthcare practitioners and patients peace of mind through innovative medical diagnostic solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Operating Officer (COO)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

About the Job
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is responsible for driving operational excellence across ISN Medical’s core business functions, ensuring efficient delivery of products and services while enabling sustainable growth and superior customer experience.
	The role requires a strategic leader who can balance immediate operational demands with long-term transformation, particularly within a regulated healthcare and medical equipment environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee and monitor operations across Supply Chain (Demand & Supply Planning, Regulatory Management, Procurement, Warehouse Management, Logistics), Sales Operations, and Customer Experience
	Ensure seamless integration and efficiency across all operational functions to support business objectives
	Drive excellence in supply chain performance and customer experience delivery
	Translate corporate strategy into clear, actionable operational execution plans
	Balance short-term operational priorities with long-term strategic growth initiatives
	Drive business growth, market expansion, and operational scalability across diverse markets
	Ensure full adherence to ISO standards (ISO 9001, ISO 13485) and regulatory requirements
	Anticipate and respond to industry trends, regulatory changes, and technological advancements
	Drive data-driven decision-making, leveraging dashboards and operational analytics
	Optimize inventory management, demand planning, procurement, and distribution efficiency
	Ensure operational resilience in delivering medical products and services in regulated environments
	Partner with Sales leadership to drive revenue growth, pricing optimization, and margin improvement
	Establish and monitor performance frameworks (KPIs, OKRs, SLAs) across all operational units
	Build and lead high-performing, cross-functional teams across operations
	Drive leadership development, capability building, and succession planning
	Ensure alignment between operational execution and commercial objectives


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, Engineering, Finance, or a related discipline. Minimum of Second Class Upper (2:1) for Bachelor’s degree holders or Upper Credit for HND holders is required.
	14 –20+ years of progressive experience in operations leadership, with at least 8 years at senior or executive management level (Director/GM/VP level).
	A Master’s degree (MBA or equivalent) is strongly preferred.
	Professional certifications in Operations, Supply Chain, Quality Management (ISO 13485), or Project Management are an advantage.
	Demonstrated experience managing multi-functional operations and P&L. Experience working in Healthcare, medical diagnostics, medical equipment, pharma or regulated environments and dealing with OEM partners
	Proven track record in driving revenue growth, margin improvement, operational scale, and customer experience excellence.


Must have skills:


	Strategic Leadership & Vision
	Operational Excellence & Process Management
	People & Leadership Development
	Financial Acumen & Resource Management
	Regulatory Compliance & Quality Assurance
	Results Orientation
	Technology & Digital Transformation
	Stakeholder Management & Communication
	Integrity and Ethics.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Credit Risk & Internal Control Officer Job at UnoCasa Limited




        
    

        
        UnoCasa is a full service Human Resources Consultancy business with focus on Talent Acquisition, Learning & Development and Consultancy services. Our raison d’etre is the notion that your people are your advantage and proffer people-centric solutions to ensure your team accomplish your business goals.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are not your run of the mill HR Company, we believe in team diversity and teamwork; we believe that the workplace should have an ambience that inspires productivity of the team; and the application of technologies to enhance efficiency. We know that every business is unique and tailor HR solutions to your organisation’s needs. Our clients are in various sectors not limited to engineering, education, health oil & gas, government, media, finance, retail, lifestyle, consultancy firms and services

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Credit Risk & Internal Control Officer

Job Location: Utako, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To Chief Risk Officer (CRO)/MD

The Role


	We are looking for a detail-oriented Credit Risk & Internal Control Officer to join our team. In this key second-line assurance role, you will safeguard the firm’s capital by identifying, assessing, and mitigating credit and operational risks while strengthening our internal control frameworks.


Key Responsibilities


	Conducting independent reviews of financing and investment exposures using financial modelling and risk grading.
	Designing and testing control frameworks to identify gaps and recommend corrective actions.
	Monitoring NPLs, defaults, and concentrations while producing high-level risk dashboards.
	Ensuring all activities align with regulatory requirements and Shariah/ethical principles.


Qualification & Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Risk Management.5–8 years in credit risk, internal audit, or financial control within the financial services or investment sector.
	Professional qualifications such as FRM, PRM, CFA, ACCA, or CIA are highly preferred.
	Strong analytical thinking, proficiency in MS Office/ERP systems, and a high level of professional integrity.


Salary
N400,000 - N600,000 per month.

Why Apply?
If you have a control-oriented mindset and the ability to challenge assumptions constructively to support sound business growth, we want to hear from you.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Management Officer (PMO)",
  "description": "Polaris Digitech Limited is a&nbsp;location intelligence firm that specializes in providing data, software and decision support services to fulfill its client&rsquo;s strategies and management needs through the development and deployment of Geographic Information Systems (GIS).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Management Officer (PMO)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Project Manager

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly skilled and tech-savvy Project Management Officer with experience in managing projects that are IT related.
	The ideal candidate will work with the Project Manager to oversee field work and follow-up with projects using project tools effective for tracking project development.


Responsibilities


	Participate in the contract review process and assist in thetranslation of the agreed
	Terms and Conditions into project deliverables.
	Effectively communicate the delegated project goals and objectives as defined by the Project Manager.
	Identify risks to projects and maintain a risk register.
	Support the development of mitigation plans, their implementation and monitoring.
	Support the Project Manager, Project Accountant and Administrative Manager in the preparation of the project status reports and internal/customer progress reports.
	Support the Project Manager, Administrative and Finance Managers regarding Purchase order maintenance, particularly regarding variation, order tracking and management ofchange.
	Comply with the company and/or project specific procedures for reporting progress andissues to satisfy customer and company requirements.
	Develop and manage project plans and reports. Participate in budget preparation foreach project working with Project Manager, Administrative Manager and Finance Manager.
	Manage and facilitate flow of essential information among company team members.
	Maintain project calendar and milestones.
	Implement and administer quantitative staffing models developed by the managementto describe staffing resource levels for projects, given their specific executionrequirements.


Qualifications and Skills


	BSc / HND in a Business Admin, Computer Science, Geography or industry related discipline.
	Maximum of 2 years of previous experience post qualification at Project Management.
	Exposure to IT solution projects in a multi-disciplinary environment.
	Sound knowledge of project management processes and the ability to deployapplications live.
	Conversant with project management metrics and evaluation.
	Good knowledge of commercial and financial processes on projects.
	Membership /Affiliate of Project Management body is an advantage or certifcation.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@polarisdigitech.net&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",

Project Management Officer (PMO) Job at Polaris Digitech Limited




        
    

        
        Polaris Digitech Limited is a location intelligence firm that specializes in providing data, software and decision support services to fulfill its client’s strategies and management needs through the development and deployment of Geographic Information Systems (GIS).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Management Officer (PMO)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Project Manager

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly skilled and tech-savvy Project Management Officer with experience in managing projects that are IT related.
	The ideal candidate will work with the Project Manager to oversee field work and follow-up with projects using project tools effective for tracking project development.


Responsibilities


	Participate in the contract review process and assist in thetranslation of the agreed
	Terms and Conditions into project deliverables.
	Effectively communicate the delegated project goals and objectives as defined by the Project Manager.
	Identify risks to projects and maintain a risk register.
	Support the development of mitigation plans, their implementation and monitoring.
	Support the Project Manager, Project Accountant and Administrative Manager in the preparation of the project status reports and internal/customer progress reports.
	Support the Project Manager, Administrative and Finance Managers regarding Purchase order maintenance, particularly regarding variation, order tracking and management ofchange.
	Comply with the company and/or project specific procedures for reporting progress andissues to satisfy customer and company requirements.
	Develop and manage project plans and reports. Participate in budget preparation foreach project working with Project Manager, Administrative Manager and Finance Manager.
	Manage and facilitate flow of essential information among company team members.
	Maintain project calendar and milestones.
	Implement and administer quantitative staffing models developed by the managementto describe staffing resource levels for projects, given their specific executionrequirements.


Qualifications and Skills


	BSc / HND in a Business Admin, Computer Science, Geography or industry related discipline.
	Maximum of 2 years of previous experience post qualification at Project Management.
	Exposure to IT solution projects in a multi-disciplinary environment.
	Sound knowledge of project management processes and the ability to deployapplications live.
	Conversant with project management metrics and evaluation.
	Good knowledge of commercial and financial processes on projects.
	Membership /Affiliate of Project Management body is an advantage or certifcation.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Executive",
  "description": "International Workplace Group (IWG) is the largest provider of flexible workspace solutions in the world. Trading under a number of brands (including Regus, Spaces, HQ, Signature and No18) we have a physical network of over 3,000 locations across 1,000 towns and cities in 120 countries. Our mission is to help our millions of customers to have a great day at work, every day.

COVID-19 forced an unprecedented experiment in flexible working, which is now driving a surge in demand for our flexible workspace solutions. To meet this demand, we are rapidly accelerating the growth of our global network. To deliver our ambition we are recruiting partners to open and operate IWG locations. Our target audience are building owners, franchise operators and competitors, we will work with our partners under a variety of commercial structures demanding a dynamic and broad-based approach to business development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Customer Service Executive

Job ID: 50587
Job Location:&nbsp;Benin City, Edo
Employment Type: Full Time (On-site)

Description


	This position sits at the heart of our growing global organisation and is a critical role where you will have an opportunity to shine and deliver world-class customer service.
	You will be part of a dynamic team with amazing career opportunities, working regular and sociable hours and with the chance to unleash your full potential.


We will expect you to:


	Give our customers and their guests a warm and friendly welcome, every day.
	Manage a range of on-site tasks to help our busy workspace run smoothly and happily.
	Above all, help your customers and colleagues have a great day at work.


About you

Customer Service Executive Job at International Workplace Group




        
    

        
        International Workplace Group (IWG) is the largest provider of flexible workspace solutions in the world. Trading under a number of brands (including Regus, Spaces, HQ, Signature and No18) we have a physical network of over 3,000 locations across 1,000 towns and cities in 120 countries. Our mission is to help our millions of customers to have a great day at work, every day.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



COVID-19 forced an unprecedented experiment in flexible working, which is now driving a surge in demand for our flexible workspace solutions. To meet this demand, we are rapidly accelerating the growth of our global network. To deliver our ambition we are recruiting partners to open and operate IWG locations. Our target audience are building owners, franchise operators and competitors, we will work with our partners under a variety of commercial structures demanding a dynamic and broad-based approach to business development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive

Job ID: 50587
Job Location: Benin City, Edo
Employment Type: Full Time (On-site)

Description


	This position sits at the heart of our growing global organisation and is a critical role where you will have an opportunity to shine and deliver world-class customer service.
	You will be part of a dynamic team with amazing career opportunities, working regular and sociable hours and with the chance to unleash your full potential.


We will expect you to:


	Give our customers and their guests a warm and friendly welcome, every day.
	Manage a range of on-site tasks to help our busy workspace run smoothly and happily.
	Above all, help your customers and colleagues have a great day at work.


About you
What really matters is that you’ve got the right mindset. We’ll provide everything else for you to build a fantastic career with IWG. All you need to bring is:


	Great communication skills and a love of interacting with people.
	A positive, outgoing and can-do attitude.
	The ability to take direction, learn new skills and apply them with confidence.
	The motivation to be your best every day – and the determination to be even better tomorrow.


What we can do for you
You are reading the right advert if you are looking for:


	A fun, challenging and rewarding career.
	Award-winning induction training and excellent ongoing learning and development.
	Fantastic promotion prospects.
	Generous, achievable incentives and sociable hours.
	Access to a comprehensive range of flexible, personalised workplace benefits that support mental, physical and financial wellbeing.
